items.title: Juiced offers $250 savings on its two Scrambler moto-style e-bikes for July 4th to new lows from $1,694
items.summary: Juiced Bikes is back in momentum and the brand has launched its 4th of July Sale that is giving folks $250 savings on its two Scrambler e-bike variant bundles. During this sale, you can find the Scrambler Moto-Style Hardtail e-bike down at $1,694 shipped, while the Scrambler Moto-Style Full Suspension e-bike is down at $1,894 shipped, and once again, both e-bikes are getting $245 FREE bundled gear packages, too. Following the usual Lectric playbook since the brand saved Juiced from financial destruction, most of the savings here are on the actual bundles rather than the e-bikes themselves, which would normally cost $1,944 and $2,144 in total, though there is the extra $5 savings we’re seeing this time around that lands the prices at new all-time lows.

items.title: Comcast’s split could make or break Peacock
items.summary: NBCUniversal executives are about to find out whether Peacock will sink or swim in the streaming industry. Now that Comcast is planning to split NBCUniversal, Peacock, and Sky from its broadband and wireless businesses, Peacock will be forced to stand on its own - without the backing of a combined company that pulled in more […]

items.title: Alienware gaming laptops are starting at $1,000 with Dell’s Back to School deals (Up to $1,980 off)
items.summary: Dell’s Back to School sale is now live with a fresh batch of discounts on a range of laptops, desktops, and accessories. You’ll also find huge discounts on Alienware gaming notebooks, including 15-inch and 16X Aurora configurations, higher-end Area-51 machines, and more, with prices starting from $999.99 shipped. You’ll find RTX 50-series options alongside some previous-generation X-series systems, with savings reaching close to $2,000. Whether you’re after a more affordable 15-inch notebook or a fully loaded Area-51 rig, this is a great chance to save on Alienware laptops you have been eyeing. Head below for a closer look at the best offers.

items.title: LEGO unveils 1,544-piece Star Wars Boba Fett figure set with iconic details + minifigure
items.summary: With it being July 1, LEGO has released the latest wave of five new building sets, and is sparing no time before unveiling the newest Star Wars set, too. We’re now getting the first looks at LEGO’s upcoming 75455 Star Wars Boba Fett, which sadly isn’t opening up for pre-order purchases, but you won’t have to wait very long to get your hands on it either. It’s joining the theme’s buildable and posable full-body figures, with some nice design elements delivering us a very crisp recreation of one of the galaxy’s most infamous bounty hunters. Head below to learn more and check out the photos.

Baseus 100W 3.3-foot Zinc Alloy USB-C cable

Buy for $4 (Reg. $8)

Apply BUWJ100W during checkout.

What we love

This 3.3-foot Baseus USB-C cable is a worthwhile upgrade for anyone tired of charging cords that fray or wear out too quickly. There’s also a slightly cheaper option for a predecessor 150W inverter for $104.26 shipped, or you can find more tool deals in the brand’s official Amazon storefront here.

items.title: Regularly $700 Cooler Master Sneaker X PC case with 360mm liquid cooler and 850W PSU just plunged 68% to $225 all-time low
items.summary: Over at Woot, as part of its wider PC components sale, you can now score the updated Cooler Master Sneaker X PC case for $224.99 Prime shipped (a $6 delivery fee will apply otherwise), with a 20% discount that auto-applies during checkout. This is the latest configuration of the case, complete with Cooler Master’s pre-installed 360mm Flux liquid cooler and an 850W V850 SFX Gold ATX 3.0 power supply. It typically sells for around $700 at Amazon, and while Woot has discounted it a few times in recent months, today’s offer marks the lowest price we have tracked yet at roughly 68% off. It is still a lot pricier than a typical PC case, but the included liquid cooler and 850W PSU make this a particularly notable value at $224.99. Head below for more details.
